rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly in the theory development, not merely in a background list. The citing authors draw on it to define and motivate key moderators (especially surcharge benefit, and related component-nature expectations) in their hypotheses about when partitioned pricing helps or hurts preference. They also include Hamilton and Srivastava (2008) as one of the empirical studies contributing observations to the meta-analysis, so it informs both framing and evidence synthesis.
relation_description: The meta-analysis treats Hamilton and Srivastava (2008) as an important prior empirical and conceptual basis for component-level moderator logic in partitioned pricing. It then builds on that basis by pooling its observations with the broader literature to estimate the relative strength and generalizability of those effects.
